expectations.text: content_ideas.json exists and parses as a JSON array of 3-4 video ideas (the weak 'wi-fi / gate times' story excluded).
expectations.evidence: Valid JSON array of 4 idea objects; wi-fi/logistics source not used (agent reported it dropped as low-value).

expectations.text: Dedup worked: exactly one idea covers the Brazil 3-0 Morocco story despite the source dump containing a duplicated Ornstein row.
expectations.evidence: Exactly one Brazil idea (source_id a1...001); agent reported the duplicate Ornstein tweet removed.

expectations.text: No idea is built from the low-value 'stadium wi-fi / gate times' source item.
Every idea has status='draft', language='ar-EG', and format='short_video'.
Every idea has a distinct time_bucket, each one of '00-06','06-12','12-18','18-24'.
Every script_segments array has 3-5 segments, and every segment duration_ms is between 6000 and 12000.
expectations.evidence: All 4 ideas have 4 segments; durations in {6000,7000,8000,9000} — within range.

expectations.text: Every script segment's text is written in Arabic script.
Each script's final segment ends with a question mark (؟ or ?) as a comment-bait CTA.
expectations.evidence: All 4 closing segments pose a question and request comments.

expectations.text: The most viral story — the Brazil 3-0 upset — is assigned the '18-24' primetime bucket.
Script text is colloquial Egyptian Arabic, free of MSA markers (هذا/الذي/سوف/لقد/الآن).

notes: Quality gaps not covered by assertions: fabricated 'id' and 'created_at' fields (these are DB-generated and should not appear in an insert payload); brief.verification set to 'partial' which is inaccurate (no verification was performed, so 'unverified' is correct). The with_skill run got both of these right.
